state.title: fd -e rs --exclude target

part.text: `fd 10.4.2` installed system-wide (not the repo's target build, but satisfies "fd tool in this repo" requirement). Run Q1-Q4.

state.title: fd -e rs --exclude target -X wc -l 2>&1 | head -3; echo "=== -x variant (one file) ==="; fd -e rs --exclude target --max-results 1 -x wc -l

part.text: Q1 + Q2 done. Now Q3 (no-ignore) and Q4 (exec wc -l on one .rs).

Q3 done — Cargo.lock + .opencode/node_modules revealed. Q4: `--max-results` incompatible with `-x`. Use plain `-x wc -l` (runs on each result) and show first line for "run on one file" — or rely on `-x` semantics.Skills doc lists `-x`/`--exec`. Re-run clean.

input.command: fd -e rs --exclude target -x wc -l | head -3; echo "=== single file pick ==="; fd -e rs --exclude target -x wc -l {} \; | sed -n '1p;3p'

state.title: fd -e rs --exclude target -x wc -l | head -3; echo "=== single file pick ==="; fd -e rs --exclude target -x wc -l {} \; | sed -n '1p;3p'

part.text:
Q4 done — `-x wc -l` executes wc -l on each result.

---

## Q1

Exact command I'd run:
```
fd -e rs --exclude target
```
Output (first 5 lines):
```
src/cli.rs
src/config.rs
src/dir_entry.rs
src/error.rs
src/exec/command.rs
```
ANSWER 1: `fd -e rs --exclude target` lists all Rust files excluding target/.

## Q2

Command:
```
fd -s README
```
Output (all matches):
```
README.md
```
ANSWER 2: `fd -s README` case-sensitive search returns only README.md.

## Q3

Command:
```
fd --no-ignore --hidden --exclude target --exclude .git
```
Shows otherwise-ignored files. Sample output:
```
CHANGELOG.md
CONTRIBUTING.md
Cargo.lock           ← normally gitignored
.opencode/node_modules/effect/dist/unstable/workflow/DurableClock.js
```
ANSWER 3: `fd --no-ignore --hidden` reveals gitignored files like Cargo.lock and .opencode/node_modules.

## Q4

Command (runs `wc -l` on each .rs result via `-x`):
```
fd -e rs --exclude target -x wc -l
```
Output (first 3 lines):
```
143 ./src/config.rs
9 ./src/error.rs
971 ./src/cli.rs
```
Note: `--max-results 1` cannot combine with `-x`/`-X` (fd errors). To process exactly one file, pipe instead: `fd ... -x wc -l | head -1`.

ANSWER 4: `fd -e rs --exclude target -x wc -l` executes `wc -l` per result, e.g. `143 ./src/config.rs`.
